source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A disk of mass $M$ and radius $R$ slides without friction on a horizontal surface. Another disk of mass $m$ and radius $r$ is pinned through its center to a point off the center of the first disk by a distance $b$, so that it can rotate without friction on the first disk as shown in Fig. 2.2. Describe the motion and identify its constants.
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: $(M+m)\dot{x}-m b \dot{\theta}\sin\theta=\text{ constant},\quad (M+m)\dot{y}+m b \dot{\theta}\cos\theta=\text{ constant},\quad \dot{\varphi}=\text{ constant},\quad T=\text{ constant},\quad \dot{\theta}=\text{ constant}$
extraction_notes: images field contains PIL object representations rather than concrete file paths or URLs

the mechanics setup combines translational and rotational variables, suggesting multi-condition reasoning

answer_type is effectively a set/list of expressions, so partial rule-based checking is feasible

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A disk of mass $M$ and radius $R$ slides without friction on a horizontal surface. Another disk of mass $m$ and radius $r$ is pinned through its center to a point off the center of the first disk by a distance $b$, so that it can rotate without friction on the first disk as shown in Fig. 2.2. Describe the motion and identify its constants.
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text: $(M+m)\dot{x}-m b \dot{\theta}\sin\theta=\text{ constant},\quad (M+m)\dot{y}+m b \dot{\theta}\cos\theta=\text{ constant},\quad \dot{\varphi}=\text{ constant},\quad T=\text{ constant},\quad \dot{\theta}=\text{ constant}$
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: images field contains PIL object representations rather than concrete file paths or URLs
metadata.reasoning_chain: Take generalized coordinates as follows: $x,y$, the coordinates of the center of mass of the larger disk, $\theta$, the angle of rotation of the larger disk and $\varphi$, the angle of rotation of the smaller disk as shown in Fig. 2.3. The center of mass of the smaller disk has coordinates $x+b\cos\theta$, $y+b\sin\theta$ and velocity components $\dot{x}-b\dot{\theta}\sin\theta$, $\dot{y}+b\dot{\theta}\cos\theta$. Hence the total kinetic energy of the system of the two disks is $T=\frac{1}{2}M(\dot{x}^{2}+\dot{y}^{2})+\frac{1}{4}MR^{2}\dot{\theta}^{2}+\frac{1}{2}m[(\dot{x}-b\dot{\theta}\sin\theta)^{2}+(\dot{y}+b\dot{\theta}\cos\theta)^{2}]+\frac{1}{4}mr^{2}\dot{\varphi}^{2}$ and the Lagrangian is $L=T=\frac{1}{2}M(\dot{x}^{2}+\dot{y}^{2})+\frac{1}{4}MR^{2}\dot{\theta}^{2}+\frac{1}{2}m[\dot{x}^{2}+\dot{y}^{2}+b^{2}\dot{\theta}^{2}-2b\dot{x}\dot{\theta}\sin\theta+2b\dot{y}\dot{\theta}\cos\theta]+\frac{1}{4}mr^{2}\dot{\varphi}^{2}$. Consider Lagrange's equations $\frac{d}{dt}(\frac{\partial L}{\partial \dot{q}_{i}})-\frac{\partial L}{\partial q_{i}}=0$. As $\frac{\partial L}{\partial x}=0$, we have $\frac{d}{dt}(\frac{\partial L}{\partial \dot{x}})=0$, so $\frac{\partial L}{\partial \dot{x}}=\text{ constant}$, or $(M+m)\dot{x}-mb\dot{\theta}\sin\theta=\text{ constant}$. Similarly, $\partial L/\partial y=0$ implies $(M+m)\dot{y}+mb\dot{\theta}\cos\theta=\text{ constant}$. Also, $\partial L/\partial \varphi=0$ leads to $\dot{\varphi}=\text{ constant}$. Finally, from $\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta}=-mb\dot{x}\dot{\theta}\cos\theta-mb\dot{y}\dot{\theta}\sin\theta$ and $\frac{\partial L}{\partial \dot{\theta}}=\frac{1}{2}MR^{2}\dot{\theta}+mb^{2}\dot{\theta}-mb\dot{x}\sin\theta+mb\dot{y}\cos\theta$, we obtain the equation of motion $\frac{1}{2}MR^{2}\ddot{\theta}+mb^{2}\ddot{\theta}-mb\ddot{x}\sin\theta+mb\ddot{y}\cos\theta=0$. Equations (1)-(4) describe the motion of the system. Since $V=0$ and $T+V=$ constant as there is no external force, the total kinetic energy of the system, $T$, is a conserved quantity. Conservation of the angular momentum about the center of mass of the system requires that, as $\dot{\varphi}=\text{ constant}$, $\dot{\theta}=\text{ constant}$.
